summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #760 in the moltis repo where schemas with nested $schema declarations caused canonicalization failures, resulting in noisy WARN-level logs on a fallback path.
summary.outcome: Added a failing test that detected the root cause (nested $schema not stripped before passing to json_schema_ast), fixed schema_normalization.rs to recursively strip $schema at all levels, and downgraded fallback warn! calls to debug!. All 367 tests pass.

repo: canonicalize_schema_for_openai_compat previously only stripped $schema from the root level, not from nested definitions/subschemas.
json_schema_ast's validate_schema_dialects recursively validates $schema at all levels, so root-only stripping is insufficient.
The project uses a 'beads' issue tracker alongside GitHub issues — issues are created, claimed, and closed as part of the fix workflow.
CLAUDE.md specifies debug! (not warn!) for expected-but-not-configured fallback states.

code.finding: Fallback warn! calls were downgraded to debug! since the fallback path is expected for non-standard schemas. $schema stripping was extended to recurse into nested definitions.

New tests added: one for nested $schema causing fallback (detecting the bug), and one verifying canonicalization side-effects (boolean-enum conversion) are applied for draft-07 schemas, not just passthrough.

workflow: Tests were written first to confirm the bug before applying the fix — the nested definitions test was iterated on to correctly distinguish fallback path from canonicalization path.
Background tasks (TaskOutput) were used to parallelize full test suite and clippy runs while verifying file state.
cargo fmt was run before clippy to keep the workflow clean.

friction: Initial test cases passed immediately via the fallback path, making it non-obvious the bug was present — required adding a canonicalization side-effect assertion to prove the fix was needed.
Multiple iterations were needed on the test to correctly detect the issue (fallback vs. canonicalization path distinction).
Locating the json_schema_ast source required several bash/grep steps to find the cargo registry path.
open_items: No explicit open items deferred — fix was complete and all tests passed.
